mission.title: qdrant: <jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size on ARM64 system
mission.description: <jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size on ARM64 system. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify qdrant troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your qdrant deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app.kubernetes.io/name=qdrant
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=qdrant -n qdrant --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review qdrant configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant qdrant configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n qdrant -l app.kubernetes.io/name=qdrant
kubectl get configmap -n qdrant -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=qdrant
```
I am trying to start `qdrant/qdrant:v1.4.1` docker image on a ARM64 machine (a QNAP NAS  with AL324 CPU and 16 Gigabyte RAM). 
Fails with memory allocation error.

## Current Behavior
Start fails with memory allocation errors.

steps.title: Apply the fix for <jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size on ARM64 system
steps.description:
Hi , does it fail on the first start?
Not sure if it is related to cpu architecture, as we test our build on ARM machines, and some part of the cloud is running on ARM.

But your device looks unusual, maybe you would need to manually disable jemalloc and build from sources
```yaml
<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size
<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size
memory allocation of 5 bytes failed
./entrypoint.sh: line 25:     7 Aborted                 ./qdrant $@
```

steps.title: Confirm <jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size on ARM64… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=qdrant -n qdrant --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n qdrant --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Hi , does it fail on the first start?
Not sure if it is related to cpu architecture, as we test our build on ARM machines, and some part of the cloud is running on ARM.

But your device looks unusual, maybe you would need to manually disable jemalloc and build from sources
codeSnippets:
<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size
<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size
memory allocation of 5 bytes failed
./entrypoint.sh: line 25:     7 Aborted                 ./qdrant $@

docker run -it qdrant/qdrant:v1.4.1
<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size
<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size
memory allocation of 5 bytes failed
./entrypoint.sh: line 25:     7 Aborted                 ./qdrant $@

docker run -it -m 8G qdrant/qdrant:v1.4.1
<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size
<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size
memory allocation of 5 bytes failed
./entrypoint.sh: line 25:     7 Aborted                 ./qdrant $@

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with qdrant installed or the issue environment reproducible.
